prompt: A photorealistic scene in a tailor's workshop. Two identical mannequins side by side. The left mannequin wears a suit that has been sewn with obsessive, excessive precision every stitch follows every tiny bump and irregularity of the mannequin's surface, pulling the fabric into contorted ridges and unnatural creases. The right mannequin wears the same suit but it fits catastrophically bunching, gaping, completely wrong. Warm workshop lighting, no text, no labels, no diagrams, cinematic detail, shallow depth of field.
metaphor: A suit tailored with obsessive precision to one mannequin's exact flaws fits perfectly on it but catastrophically fails on an identical second mannequin, showing how over-specificity prevents generalization.
